name: Bucatini All' Amatriciana
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 Tablespoons minced fresh chili pepper, seeds removed
1/2 Cup sliced fresh basil leaves, plus 4 sprigs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 3-quart non-reactive saucepan, heat the tomatoes over medium heat until juicy, about 5 minutes.
2. Pass the tomatoes through a food mill and puree. Set aside.
3. Wipe the saucepan clean and return to medium heat. Add olive oil and pancetta; cook, stirring for about 15 minutes or until crisp.
4. Remove the pancetta with a slotted spoon and reserve. Pour off all but about 2 tablespoons of fat from the pan.
5. Add onions and cook over medium heat until golden, about 5 minutes.
6. Add white wine and reduce by three-quarters.
7. Add reserved tomatoes, chili pepper, and red pepper flakes. Simmer gently for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
8. Add reserved pancetta, basil, and half of the Pecorino Romano. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for an additional 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
9. Bring a gallon of water to a boil and add 1 tablespoon of salt. Cook the Bucatini until al dente. Drain and toss with the sauce.
10. Pour into a warmed serving bowl and sprinkle with remaining cheese. Garnish with basil sprigs.
Yield: 4 servings
